response.justification: The candidate exceeds every technical requirement — strong TypeScript/Node.js, React exposure, REST and web architecture depth, Kubernetes, AWS CDK, and current hands-on LLM/MCP integration work that hits the nice-to-haves. However, this is a staff-level engineer with roughly 19 years of experience applying to an entry-level junior/mid role, which raises serious concerns about compensation fit, retention, and role satisfaction. PostgreSQL specifically is not evidenced (MongoDB/MySQL instead), and basic German may matter for a German renovation-industry platform. Worth a conversation only if the candidate knowingly wants a scope-down.
strengths: Deep fullstack TypeScript/Node.js experience with React and AWS in production
Current LLM/MCP and AI-agent tooling work matches the nice-to-have qualifications
Kubernetes, Docker, and CI/CD experience plus a strong testing/craftsmanship culture
concerns: Severely overqualified for an entry-level role — retention and salary-fit risk
No explicit PostgreSQL or Nest.js experience; recent DB work is MongoDB
Only basic German for a high on-site role serving German energy/renovation workflows

key_factors.factor: Technical stack coverage (TypeScript, React, Node, AWS)
